# UC-13.4.4 · LLM Model Version Deployment Tracking, Cost-and-Behavior Drift Attribution, and Inadvertent Provider-Side Upgrade Detection

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Configuration, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We keep a dated name tag on the exact recipe version answering your automated helpers, like noting which edition of a cookbook a chef used. When the publisher swaps pages under the same cover, or a taste test grows bigger than agreed, we raise a clear flag so stewards can undo it calmly.*

---

## Description

Detects which dated LLM snapshots and foundation model identifiers actually serve production traffic across OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI deployments behind gateways, and LiteLLM routes—surfacing unpinned aliases that float when providers rotate underlying weights, shadow or canary slices that exceed approved percentages, fingerprint and snapshot churn correlated with cost-per-thousand-token steps, and configuration gaps versus an allowlisted regex ledger for governance rollback decisions.

## Value

AI governance, audit, and platform leaders receive a tamper-evident ledger that ties live traffic to explicit snapshot identities so silent provider-side upgrades, accidental shadow scale-ups, and behavioral regressions become explainable before customers, regulators, or finance teams discover them through incidents; engineering gains routing tokens that accelerate rollback to a board-approved model card without conflating the story with latency SLOs, invoice-only cost dashboards, gateway throttle fairness, or internal MLTK drift math.

## Implementation

Land openai:chat:completion, anthropic:messages, google:gemini:generate, bedrock:invocation, and gateway:litellm lines into Splunk; publish llm_approved_model_versions.csv with regex pins and shadow caps; normalize tenant_id, deployment_name, model_alias, resolved_snapshot, system_fingerprint, tokens, and cost_usd; save platform_uc_13_4_4_llm_model_version_deployment_tracking every fifteen minutes with earliest=-24h@h; route critical severities to AI governance on-call; archive weekly CSV evidence packs with ver_min and ver_max tuples.

## Evidence

Saved search platform_uc_13_4_4_llm_model_version_deployment_tracking on a fifteen-minute schedule; dashboard drilldowns across gateway and provider sourcetypes; alert routing to AI governance on-call; weekly CSV exports of ver_min, ver_max, and change_touchpoints archived to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab tenant, route traffic through an unpinned alias while alias_pin_required=yes in llm_approved_model_versions.csv and forward all five multisearch lanes, confirm platform_uc_13_4_4_llm_model_version_deployment_tracking returns critical with floating_alias within two fifteen-minute buckets.

### Negative scenario

After restoring pinned snapshots and weights that match approved_snapshot_regex, when ver_dc stays at one for steady traffic and shadow percentages remain within policy, confirm severities return to info or low and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with ML platform engineering, AI governance and model risk stewards, API gateway operators, cloud FinOps partners who maintain approved price books, identity and access management, application security, and observability engineers who operate HTTP Event Collector landing zones plus OpenTelemetry Collector agents on inference paths. This use case is deliberately not UC-13.4.1 managed LLM latency and HTTP error fusion, not UC-13.4.2 token usage and quota burn economics, not UC-13.4.5 gateway rate-limit and fairness analytics, and not UC-13.4.15 Splunk MLTK internal model drift statistics. Here the evidence story is which dated provider snapshot or foundation model identifier actually answers production prompts after aliases such as gpt-4o or claude-3-sonnet silently float forward, whether canary or shadow routes bleed past their intended five percent slice toward fifty percent, whether OpenAI system_fingerprint churn, Anthropic dated model ids, Google Gemini versioned publishers, Azure OpenAI deployment revisions, AWS Bedrock foundation model arns, LiteLLM proxy weight maps, or internal Llama and Mistral fine-tune image tags changed without a matching change ticket, and whether marginal cost per thousand tokens or safety evaluation scores step-change in the same window so auditors can separate provider-side rotation from application bugs.

Provider semantics differ materially. OpenAI chat completions return a model string that may reflect an alias while system_fingerprint captures runtime snapshot variance; you must log both request model and response model together with api_version to reconstruct pinning posture. Anthropic Messages API expects explicit model ids such as claude-sonnet-4-20250514 and will not silently remap a fully qualified id, yet client libraries may still send shorthand labels that your gateway expands—log pre- and post-router identifiers. Google Gemini publishes versioned model codes such as gemini-2.0-flash-001 with lifecycle states; Vertex AI locations and publisher endpoints must accompany model ids or joins confuse regional rollouts with version swaps. Azure OpenAI binds customer traffic to deployment_name while the underlying model version changes when operators update the deployment resource; ingest Azure Resource Manager change streams or deployment metadata snapshots alongside runtime diagnostics. Amazon Bedrock identifies models with foundation model ids and inference profiles; log the exact modelId from InvokeModel responses and any applied guardrail Amazon Resource Names. LiteLLM and similar proxies add team metadata, routed_model, and optional cache hits; treat the proxy as authoritative for effective routing when applications omit provider headers.

RBAC is mandatory. Automation that reads approved version lookups, CMDB rows, and gateway Git repositories must use workload identities with read-only scope, not personal administrator tokens on laptops. Legal and privacy reviewers must decide whether prompts, completions, or customer identifiers may appear in gateway logs; when redaction is mandatory, retain model metadata, token counts, hashes of prompts for differential debugging only where policy allows, and never store raw secrets. Network architects should confirm collectors reach provider endpoints through approved egress without TLS interception that breaks certificate pinning on canary probes.

Index design lands complementary sourcetypes without forcing one parser to pretend it understands every vendor. Typical patterns include index=ai_proxy with sourcetype=openai:chat:completion for OpenAI-compatible completion JSON including usage blocks, index=ai_proxy with sourcetype=anthropic:messages for Anthropic message completions, index=ai_vertex or index=gcp_ai with sourcetype=google:gemini:generate for Vertex or AI Studio structured logs, index=aws_bedrock with sourcetype=bedrock:invocation for InvokeModel and Converse narratives, and index=ai_gateway with sourcetype=gateway:litellm for unified proxy rows that also surface Azure OpenAI deployments and Bedrock profiles behind one edge. Retention should cover at least thirty days for governance attestations and ninety days when regulators expect quarterly replay evidence. Stamp change_ticket_id on maintenance that touches router weights, default model environment variables, prompt template catalogs, or safety evaluator schedules so validators correlate benign drift.

Field normalization expectations: tenant_id is a stable billing or security boundary such as Entra tenant short code, Google Cloud project number, AWS organization account alias, or internal chargeback key. deployment_name is the Azure OpenAI deployment, Bedrock inference profile, LiteLLM route name, or internal service cluster—not merely a Kubernetes Deployment object unless you explicitly map it. model_alias is the user-facing or SDK-facing string before routing. model_version is any explicit dated snapshot field your gateway or provider returns. system_fingerprint is OpenAI-specific runtime lineage when present. region is the serving geography. total_tokens, prompt_tokens, and completion_tokens must be integers suitable for behavioral and economic attribution; cost_usd may be estimated from FinOps tables at ingest or forwarded from vendor billing hooks when permitted.

Risk briefing: silent alias rotation can improve quality yet break deterministic evaluations, void regression baselines, and invalidate signed model cards overnight. Shadow traffic that overshoots planned percentage violates AI governance gates and can leak prerelease behaviors to regulated users. Cost per thousand tokens may jump when a router promotes a larger context window class even though the alias string looks unchanged. Safety regressions often correlate with snapshot hops; pair this control with offline evaluation feeds rather than pretending Splunk replaces red-team harnesses.

Differentiation recap: resolved snapshot ledger, alias pinning hygiene, shadow percentage governance, provider silent upgrade detection, rollback evidence for model governance committees—not raw latency percentiles, not monthly invoice reconciliation alone, not MLTK PSI drift, not gateway 429 fairness.

FinOps linkage: cost_usd fields here contextualize version steps; authoritative spend remains UC-13.4.2. When FinOps refreshes list prices, update notes in llm_approved_model_versions.csv so investigators interpret cost_step_ratio without false blame.

SLO companion: mirror summarized tuples into a summary index keyed on tenant_id, deployment_name, and model_alias for quarterly model governance council reviews when executives ask whether production traffic still runs the snapshot board members approved.

Governance cadence: weekly office hours between platform engineering and legal should review new provider announcements, reconcile them with approved_snapshot_regex rows, and decide whether floating aliases remain acceptable for sandbox tenants only. Monthly internal audits should replay ver_max distributions for regulated workloads and attach Splunk exports to change advisory board minutes. Quarterly tabletop exercises should assume an inadvertent snapshot hop during a peak retail event and practice rollback using this detection as the primary timeline source.

Training expectations: developers must understand that model_alias strings in application configuration are not sufficient audit evidence without resolved_snapshot or system_fingerprint fields; onboarding modules should cite this use case when teams first receive API keys. Support engineers should know how to open a drilldown from severity rows to raw gateway JSON without exporting customer prompts to insecure channels.

Tooling integrations: when Splunk SOAR or ticketing webhooks consume this alert, map recommended_action tokens to runbook URLs stored in the notes column of llm_approved_model_versions.csv so machines and humans share the same routing vocabulary. ServiceNow change records should reference tenant_id and deployment_name fields to auto-close benign alerts when maintenance windows align.

Evidence integrity: sign lookup files at commit time or store sha256 metadata in a small Splunk KV collection so investigators detect tampering with approved regex rows. Separate indexes for production and non-production reduce the chance that lab alias experiments pollute executive dashboards.

Capacity planning: high-cardinality system_fingerprint values can expand index sizes; consider sampling long-tail fingerprints into a bucket label while retaining full fidelity for top revenue tenants. Search concurrency limits may require moving the heaviest stats arms to summary indexes during Black Friday style peaks.

Vendor liaison: designate named contacts for OpenAI, Microsoft, Google, and AWS who receive monthly ver_dc summaries and can escalate suspected silent rotations with vendor support tickets referencing timestamps from this control.

Closing prerequisite checklist: confirm gateway exporters emit dated ids where required, Azure deployment inventory jobs exist, Bedrock profile catalogs are current, LiteLLM config mirrors Git, and llm_approved_model_versions.csv reflects this week’s provider announcements.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes the multisearch fans wide, plus CSV governance artifacts that keep approved snapshot regular expressions, shadow caps, and owner teams out of SPL literals.

Lane A, OpenAI chat completions: configure proxies or middleware to emit sourcetype=openai:chat:completion JSON with model, response model, system_fingerprint when returned, api_version, usage.prompt_tokens, usage.completion_tokens, usage.total_tokens, request_id, tenant headers, and region hints. Preserve raw model_alias strings without normalizing away trailing snapshot tokens.

Lane B, Anthropic Messages: land sourcetype=anthropic:messages with input and output token fields, explicit model ids after gateway expansion, organization or workspace identifiers, and hashed API key fingerprints for attribution without secrets.

Lane C, Google Gemini generation: stream Vertex AI or AI Studio diagnostics into sourcetype=google:gemini:generate with publisher model strings, location, project identifiers, token usage, and safety block flags when policy permits.

Lane D, Amazon Bedrock invocations: forward InvokeModel and Converse logs as sourcetype=bedrock:invocation including modelId, inference profile arn, Region, input and output token counts, and guardrail invocation metadata.

Lane E, LiteLLM gateway: standardize sourcetype=gateway:litellm lines with team_id, deployment_name mirrors for Azure OpenAI, Bedrock profile names, routed_model, optional system_fingerprint passthrough, cache_hit, retry_count, and estimated cost_usd when the proxy calculates burn.

Lookup maintenance: publish llm_approved_model_versions.csv with columns tenant_id, deployment_name, provider, model_alias, approved_snapshot_regex, max_shadow_traffic_pct, alias_pin_required, owner_team, notes. Refresh within one business day of provider announcements. Version the file in Git with pull requests tied to CAB records.

Example curl helpers from hardened bastions with scoped tokens:

curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_ADMIN" "https://api.openai.com/v1/models" | head
curl -fsS -H "x-api-key: $ANTHROPIC_KEY" "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages" -d '{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-20250514","max_tokens":16,"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"ping"}]}' | head
gcloud ai models list --region=us-central1 | head
aws bedrock list-foundation-models --region us-east-1 | head

props.conf guidance: set TRUNCATE safely for wide JSON, honor provider timestamps in TIME_PREFIX, FIELDALIAS alternate token field names, and SEDCMD or INGEST_EVAL to drop prompt bodies when forbidden while retaining model metadata.

Pre-save validation: confirm each sourcetype returns rows in the last hour during business traffic, that a synthetic alias-only request in lab flags floating_alias when alias_pin_required=yes in the lookup, and that allowlist joins resolve owner_team without null spikes.

Dashboards: host a matrix by tenant_id and deployment_name with color thresholds from severity, drilldowns to raw multisearch lanes, allowlist rows, and Change datamodel touchpoint counts.

Ingest hardening: duplicate HEC tokens on blue-green forwarders should include a host or forwarder fingerprint field so deduplication logic can ignore mirrored copies of the same request_id. Clock skew beyond ninety seconds between gateway and Splunk indexer time can misplace flip_flop detection; monitor NTP on all collection tiers weekly.

Schema contracts: publish protobuf or JSON schema definitions for each lane in your internal developer portal so mobile and web clients cannot silently rename model fields during releases. Breaking schema changes require a minor version bump and parser updates before production rollout.

Canary probes: schedule synthetic calls that always request pinned snapshots from five regions; land them under a dedicated sourcetype or tenant_id=canary_probe so they never pollute production traffic_share_pct while still validating end-to-end logging.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_4_4_llm_model_version_deployment_tracking with a fifteen-minute schedule during steady production, dispatch.earliest_time=-24h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical, high, or medium after deduplication depending on governance policy. Throttle duplicate tenant_id, deployment_name, provider, model_alias tuples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from medium to critical. Include recommended_action, ver_max, traffic_share_pct, and flip_flop hints in pager bodies so responders open gateway Git history, Azure deployment blades, Bedrock profile consoles, and evaluator dashboards without guesswork.

Pipeline narrative unlike UC-13.4.1 percentile latency fusion: multisearch merges OpenAI chat completion, Anthropic Messages, Google Gemini generation, Bedrock invocation, and LiteLLM gateway lanes so a silent single vendor feed cannot hide a shadow bleed or alias float. Quarter-hour bucketing aligns traffic_share_pct math before stats computes distinct resolved_snapshot counts, min and max snapshot lexicographic anchors, token sums, and cost_per_1k proxies. streamstats compares ver_max across recent buckets per tuple to raise flip_flop when providers rotate fingerprints or snapshot ids. eventstats derives slice_total for percentage governance relative to sibling traffic in the same tenant and deployment slice. join enriches llm_approved_model_versions.csv for approved_snapshot_regex, shadow caps, and owner_team. A second join executes tstats against the Splunk CIM Change datamodel counting Change.user touchpoints as coarse configuration churn context for the tenant key—this is not a substitute for full change records but detaches silent upgrades from weeks of zero ticketing. case maps mismatch_allowlist, floating_alias, shadow_breach, flip_flop, and cost_step_ratio into severity tiers. coalesce ladders absorb renamed fields across exporters. Closing table presents analyst-visible columns including lanes and change_touchpoints for audit exports.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.4 LLM resolved snapshot pinning shadow-traffic governance silent provider rotation fingerprint drift Tunables earliest=-24h@h bucket=15m shadow_slack=1.5 cost_jump=1.35")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=ai_proxy sourcetype=openai:chat:completion earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval provider="openai_public"
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, organization_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval deployment_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_name, openai_project, "openai_default"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, datacenter_region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, response_model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval model_alias=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_alias, request_model_alias, model))))
      | eval model_version=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_version, openai_snapshot, ""))))
      | eval system_fingerprint=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(system_fingerprint, response_system_fingerprint, ""))))
      | eval resolved_snapshot=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(if(len(model_version)==0,null(),model_version), system_fingerprint, model, "unresolved"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens, usage_prompt_tokens, 0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens, usage_completion_tokens, 0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens, usage_total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens, 0))
      | eval cost_usd=tonumber(coalesce(cost_usd, charged_usd, 0))
      | eval lane="openai_chat_completion_lane"
      | fields _time tenant_id provider deployment_name region model model_alias model_version system_fingerprint resolved_snapshot prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens cost_usd lane ]
    [ search index=ai_proxy sourcetype=anthropic:messages earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval provider="anthropic_messages"
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, anthropic_organization_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval deployment_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_name, workspace_slug, "anthropic_default"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, anthropic_model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval model_alias=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_alias, requested_model, model))))
      | eval model_version=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_version, claude_snapshot_id, ""))))
      | eval system_fingerprint=""
      | eval resolved_snapshot=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(if(len(model_version)==0,null(),model_version), model, "unresolved"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens, input_tokens, 0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens, output_tokens, 0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens, 0))
      | eval cost_usd=tonumber(coalesce(cost_usd, usage_cost_usd, 0))
      | eval lane="anthropic_messages_lane"
      | fields _time tenant_id provider deployment_name region model model_alias model_version system_fingerprint resolved_snapshot prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens cost_usd lane ]
    [ search index=ai_vertex sourcetype=google:gemini:generate earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval provider="google_gemini"
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, gcp_project_number, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval deployment_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_name, publisher_endpoint, "gemini_default"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, vertex_location, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, gemini_model_id, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval model_alias=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_alias, routed_model, model))))
      | eval model_version=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_version, gemini_served_revision, ""))))
      | eval system_fingerprint=""
      | eval resolved_snapshot=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(if(len(model_version)==0,null(),model_version), model, "unresolved"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens, prompt_token_count, 0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens, candidates_token_count, 0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens, 0))
      | eval cost_usd=tonumber(coalesce(cost_usd, 0))
      | eval lane="google_gemini_generate_lane"
      | fields _time tenant_id provider deployment_name region model model_alias model_version system_fingerprint resolved_snapshot prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens cost_usd lane ]
    [ search index=aws_bedrock sourcetype=bedrock:invocation earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval provider="aws_bedrock"
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, aws_account_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval deployment_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_name, inference_profile_id, foundation_model_arn, "bedrock_default"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, aws_region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, model_id, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval model_alias=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_alias, requested_model_id, model))))
      | eval model_version=model
      | eval system_fingerprint=""
      | eval resolved_snapshot=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_id, model, "unresolved"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens, input_token_count, 0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens, output_token_count, 0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens, 0))
      | eval cost_usd=tonumber(coalesce(cost_usd, invocation_cost_usd, 0))
      | eval lane="bedrock_invocation_lane"
      | fields _time tenant_id provider deployment_name region model model_alias model_version system_fingerprint resolved_snapshot prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens cost_usd lane ]
    [ search index=ai_gateway sourcetype=gateway:litellm earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval provider=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(custom_llm_provider, litellm_custom_llm_provider, "litellm_proxy"))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, team_id, key_alias, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval deployment_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_name, azure_openai_deployment, aws_bedrock_profile, "litellm_route"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, endpoint_region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, litellm_model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval model_alias=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_alias, user_requested_model, model))))
      | eval model_version=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_version, api_snapshot_label, ""))))
      | eval system_fingerprint=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(system_fingerprint, openai_system_fingerprint, ""))))
      | eval resolved_snapshot=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(if(len(model_version)==0,null(),model_version), system_fingerprint, model, "unresolved"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens, proxy_prompt_tokens, 0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens, proxy_completion_tokens, 0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens, proxy_total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens, 0))
      | eval cost_usd=tonumber(coalesce(cost_usd, litellm_cost_usd, 0))
      | eval lane="gateway_litellm_lane"
      | fields _time tenant_id provider deployment_name region model model_alias model_version system_fingerprint resolved_snapshot prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens cost_usd lane ]
| eval tenant_id=coalesce(tenant_id,"unknown_tenant")
| eval deployment_name=coalesce(deployment_name,"default_route")
| eval model_alias=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_alias,"unknown_alias"))))
| eval resolved_snapshot=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(resolved_snapshot, model_version, system_fingerprint, model_alias, "unresolved"))))
| bucket _time span=15m
| stats count AS req_count sum(total_tokens) AS sum_tokens sum(cost_usd) AS sum_cost dc(resolved_snapshot) AS ver_dc values(resolved_snapshot) AS ver_vals min(resolved_snapshot) AS ver_min max(resolved_snapshot) AS ver_max values(lane) AS lanes values(system_fingerprint) AS fp_vals BY _time tenant_id provider deployment_name region model_alias
| sort tenant_id deployment_name provider model_alias + _time
| streamstats window=6 current=t global=f last(ver_max) AS prev_ver_max BY tenant_id deployment_name provider model_alias
| eval flip_flop=if(ver_dc>1 OR (isnotnull(prev_ver_max) AND len(prev_ver_max)>0 AND prev_ver_max!=ver_max),1,0)
| eventstats sum(req_count) AS slice_total BY tenant_id deployment_name _time
| eval traffic_share_pct=if(slice_total>0, round(100*req_count/slice_total,2), 0)
| eval cost_per_1k=if(sum_tokens>0, round(1000*sum_cost/sum_tokens,6), 0)
| streamstats window=4 current=t global=f last(cost_per_1k) AS prev_cp1k BY tenant_id deployment_name provider model_alias
| eval cost_step_ratio=if(isnotnull(prev_cp1k) AND prev_cp1k>0.000001, round(cost_per_1k/prev_cp1k,3), 0)
| join type=left max=0 tenant_id deployment_name provider model_alias
    [| inputlookup llm_approved_model_versions.csv
     | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(tenant_id)))
     | eval deployment_name=lower(trim(toString(deployment_name)))
     | eval provider=lower(trim(toString(provider)))
     | eval model_alias=lower(trim(toString(model_alias)))
     | fields tenant_id deployment_name provider model_alias approved_snapshot_regex max_shadow_traffic_pct alias_pin_required owner_team notes ]
| eval approved_snapshot_regex=coalesce(approved_snapshot_regex,".*")
| eval max_shadow_traffic_pct=tonumber(coalesce(max_shadow_traffic_pct,100))
| eval alias_pin_required=coalesce(alias_pin_required,"no")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned_route")
| eval allow_match=if(match(ver_max, approved_snapshot_regex),1,0)
| eval mismatch_allowlist=if(allow_match==0 AND approved_snapshot_regex!=".*",1,0)
| eval floating_alias=if(alias_pin_required=="yes" AND match(model_alias,"^(gpt-4o|gpt-4-turbo|claude-3-sonnet|gemini-2\.0-flash|gemini-1\.5-pro)$"),1,0)
| eval shadow_breach=if(max_shadow_traffic_pct<99 AND traffic_share_pct>(max_shadow_traffic_pct*1.5),1,0)
| join type=left max=0 tenant_id
    [| tstats count AS change_touchpoints FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Change.user
     | rename Change.user AS tenant_id
     | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(tenant_id))) ]
| eval severity=case(mismatch_allowlist==1 OR floating_alias==1, "critical", shadow_breach==1 OR flip_flop==1 OR cost_step_ratio>1.35, "high", cost_step_ratio>1.15 OR ver_dc>1, "medium", traffic_share_pct>8 AND traffic_share_pct<=max_shadow_traffic_pct*1.2, "low", true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical" AND floating_alias==1, "pin_dated_snapshots_freeze_alias_routes_update_gateway_config_repo", severity=="critical" AND mismatch_allowlist==1, "rollback_traffic_refresh_allowlist_open_model_governance_ticket", severity=="high" AND flip_flop==1, "correlate_openai_system_fingerprint_and_provider_release_calendar_stabilize_route", severity=="high" AND shadow_breach==1, "rebalance_litellm_weight_map_validate_azure_deployment_and_bedrock_profile_targets", severity=="high", "diff_prompt_templates_evaluate_safety_regressions_before_cost_chasing", true(), "continue_hourly_version_ledger_exports_for_audit")
| table _time tenant_id owner_team provider deployment_name region model_alias ver_min ver_max ver_dc traffic_share_pct cost_per_1k cost_step_ratio flip_flop mismatch_allowlist floating_alias shadow_breach severity recommended_action lanes change_touchpoints
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[platform_uc_13_4_4_llm_model_version_deployment_tracking_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-ai-governance@example.com
action.email.subject = LLM model version $result.severity$ tenant=$result.tenant_id$ route=$result.deployment_name$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_4_4_llm_model_version_deployment_tracking | where severity IN ("critical","high","medium")

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above objectives, materialize per-arm quarter-hour summaries into summary indexes keyed on tenant_id, deployment_name, provider, and model_alias, widen alert windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. For extremely high-cardinality fingerprints, collapse rare tails through a lookup tier column before indexing.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in provider consoles and Git-backed gateway configs: compare OpenAI project model settings, Anthropic workspace defaults, Vertex AI model availability tables, Azure OpenAI deployment resource versions, AWS Bedrock foundation model access, and LiteLLM config.yaml route maps against Splunk ver_max for the same fifteen-minute bucket. When Splunk lags, verify HEC token health, OpenTelemetry exporter backpressure, and CloudWatch subscription consumer lag before muting detections.

Deep dive when mismatch_allowlist triggers: pull exemplar request identifiers from gateway logs, confirm whether clients sent dated ids or aliases, replay through a pinned test client in non-production, and diff Terraform or Helm values that set default model environment variables.

Shadow validation: during an approved five percent canary, confirm traffic_share_pct stays within slack of max_shadow_traffic_pct for two hours; intentionally raise weight in lab and confirm shadow_breach flips within one bucket.

Fingerprint validation: capture two OpenAI responses minutes apart during a known provider maintenance window; confirm system_fingerprint changes appear in fp_vals while model_alias remains constant, and that flip_flop or severity reflects your governance threshold.

Negative test: after rollback when only approved_snapshot_regex snapshots serve traffic, aliases are pinned, and weights match change records, confirm filtering severity to critical and high returns zero rows for six consecutive runs.

Runbook linkage: document planned provider deprecations and Gemini lifecycle milestones so validators do not file defects during advertised snapshot sunsets.

Cross-vendor checks: when a single tenant spans OpenAI and Bedrock failovers, validate that deployment_name normalization keeps traffic_share_pct meaningful and does not double-count mirrored requests reflected through both direct and proxy lanes.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — OpenAI alias gpt-4o silently maps to a new snapshot: pin gpt-4o-2024-11-20 in gateway config, refresh evaluator baselines, update llm_approved_model_versions.csv regex, and publish an executive note that deterministic replay hashes changed.

Case 2 — Anthropic dated id drift after workspace policy update: reconcile admin console defaults with Splunk ver_max, revoke shorthand client strings, and require explicit claude snapshot ids in CI lint rules.

Case 3 — Gemini publisher rolls gemini-2.0-flash-001 lifecycle state: follow Google release notes, shift traffic to the replacement id under change control, and annotate notes column in the allowlist before muting alerts.

Case 4 — Azure OpenAI deployment resource bumped while deployment_name stayed constant: ingest Azure Activity Log Change events into the Change datamodel arm, correlate with cost_step_ratio, and snapshot deployment model version properties weekly.

Case 5 — Bedrock inference profile now targets a different foundation model arn: diff profile configurations in infrastructure-as-code, confirm Splunk resolved_snapshot matches expected arn tail, and validate guardrail associations still bind.

Case 6 — LiteLLM weight map accidentally promotes shadow to majority traffic: restore Git revision, enforce code review on weight keys, and tie platform_uc_13_4_4 alerts to deployment pipelines.

Case 7 — Internal Llama fine-tune container image tag moves without ticket: join Kubernetes image digest metadata in a companion search, block deploys missing change_ticket_id labels, and roll back image.

Case 8 — Cost per thousand tokens jumps after snapshot hop: separate UC-13.4.2 invoice reconciliation from this version narrative, confirm price book rows, and inspect context window utilization—not only token price tables.

Case 9 — Safety regression correlated with ver_dc greater than one: freeze promotion, run offline red-team suites against both snapshots, and engage policy owners before re-enabling traffic.

Case 10 — False calm when HEC lane drops: if openai_chat_completion_lane goes quiet while traffic continues, fix forwarders before trusting negative results.

Case 11 — Duplicate events double traffic_share_pct: dedupe on request_id at ingest or lower severity until dedupe summaries land.

Case 12 — Quarterly audit export: archive fifteen-minute tuples with ver_min, ver_max, and change_touchpoints to restricted indexes for AI governance sign-off.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.4 LLM resolved snapshot pinning shadow-traffic governance silent provider rotation fingerprint drift Tunables earliest=-24h@h bucket=15m shadow_slack=1.5 cost_jump=1.35")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=ai_proxy sourcetype=openai:chat:completion earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval provider="openai_public"
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, organization_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval deployment_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_name, openai_project, "openai_default"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, datacenter_region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, response_model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval model_alias=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_alias, request_model_alias, model))))
      | eval model_version=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_version, openai_snapshot, ""))))
      | eval system_fingerprint=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(system_fingerprint, response_system_fingerprint, ""))))
      | eval resolved_snapshot=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(if(len(model_version)==0,null(),model_version), system_fingerprint, model, "unresolved"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens, usage_prompt_tokens, 0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens, usage_completion_tokens, 0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens, usage_total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens, 0))
      | eval cost_usd=tonumber(coalesce(cost_usd, charged_usd, 0))
      | eval lane="openai_chat_completion_lane"
      | fields _time tenant_id provider deployment_name region model model_alias model_version system_fingerprint resolved_snapshot prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens cost_usd lane ]
    [ search index=ai_proxy sourcetype=anthropic:messages earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval provider="anthropic_messages"
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, anthropic_organization_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval deployment_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_name, workspace_slug, "anthropic_default"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, anthropic_model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval model_alias=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_alias, requested_model, model))))
      | eval model_version=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_version, claude_snapshot_id, ""))))
      | eval system_fingerprint=""
      | eval resolved_snapshot=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(if(len(model_version)==0,null(),model_version), model, "unresolved"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens, input_tokens, 0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens, output_tokens, 0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens, 0))
      | eval cost_usd=tonumber(coalesce(cost_usd, usage_cost_usd, 0))
      | eval lane="anthropic_messages_lane"
      | fields _time tenant_id provider deployment_name region model model_alias model_version system_fingerprint resolved_snapshot prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens cost_usd lane ]
    [ search index=ai_vertex sourcetype=google:gemini:generate earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval provider="google_gemini"
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, gcp_project_number, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval deployment_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_name, publisher_endpoint, "gemini_default"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, vertex_location, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, gemini_model_id, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval model_alias=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_alias, routed_model, model))))
      | eval model_version=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_version, gemini_served_revision, ""))))
      | eval system_fingerprint=""
      | eval resolved_snapshot=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(if(len(model_version)==0,null(),model_version), model, "unresolved"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens, prompt_token_count, 0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens, candidates_token_count, 0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens, 0))
      | eval cost_usd=tonumber(coalesce(cost_usd, 0))
      | eval lane="google_gemini_generate_lane"
      | fields _time tenant_id provider deployment_name region model model_alias model_version system_fingerprint resolved_snapshot prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens cost_usd lane ]
    [ search index=aws_bedrock sourcetype=bedrock:invocation earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval provider="aws_bedrock"
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, aws_account_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval deployment_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_name, inference_profile_id, foundation_model_arn, "bedrock_default"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, aws_region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, model_id, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval model_alias=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_alias, requested_model_id, model))))
      | eval model_version=model
      | eval system_fingerprint=""
      | eval resolved_snapshot=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_id, model, "unresolved"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens, input_token_count, 0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens, output_token_count, 0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens, 0))
      | eval cost_usd=tonumber(coalesce(cost_usd, invocation_cost_usd, 0))
      | eval lane="bedrock_invocation_lane"
      | fields _time tenant_id provider deployment_name region model model_alias model_version system_fingerprint resolved_snapshot prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens cost_usd lane ]
    [ search index=ai_gateway sourcetype=gateway:litellm earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval provider=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(custom_llm_provider, litellm_custom_llm_provider, "litellm_proxy"))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, team_id, key_alias, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval deployment_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_name, azure_openai_deployment, aws_bedrock_profile, "litellm_route"))))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, endpoint_region, "unknown_region"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, litellm_model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval model_alias=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_alias, user_requested_model, model))))
      | eval model_version=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_version, api_snapshot_label, ""))))
      | eval system_fingerprint=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(system_fingerprint, openai_system_fingerprint, ""))))
      | eval resolved_snapshot=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(if(len(model_version)==0,null(),model_version), system_fingerprint, model, "unresolved"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(prompt_tokens, proxy_prompt_tokens, 0))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(completion_tokens, proxy_completion_tokens, 0))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(coalesce(total_tokens, proxy_total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens, 0))
      | eval cost_usd=tonumber(coalesce(cost_usd, litellm_cost_usd, 0))
      | eval lane="gateway_litellm_lane"
      | fields _time tenant_id provider deployment_name region model model_alias model_version system_fingerprint resolved_snapshot prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens cost_usd lane ]
| eval tenant_id=coalesce(tenant_id,"unknown_tenant")
| eval deployment_name=coalesce(deployment_name,"default_route")
| eval model_alias=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_alias,"unknown_alias"))))
| eval resolved_snapshot=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(resolved_snapshot, model_version, system_fingerprint, model_alias, "unresolved"))))
| bucket _time span=15m
| stats count AS req_count sum(total_tokens) AS sum_tokens sum(cost_usd) AS sum_cost dc(resolved_snapshot) AS ver_dc values(resolved_snapshot) AS ver_vals min(resolved_snapshot) AS ver_min max(resolved_snapshot) AS ver_max values(lane) AS lanes values(system_fingerprint) AS fp_vals BY _time tenant_id provider deployment_name region model_alias
| sort tenant_id deployment_name provider model_alias + _time
| streamstats window=6 current=t global=f last(ver_max) AS prev_ver_max BY tenant_id deployment_name provider model_alias
| eval flip_flop=if(ver_dc>1 OR (isnotnull(prev_ver_max) AND len(prev_ver_max)>0 AND prev_ver_max!=ver_max),1,0)
| eventstats sum(req_count) AS slice_total BY tenant_id deployment_name _time
| eval traffic_share_pct=if(slice_total>0, round(100*req_count/slice_total,2), 0)
| eval cost_per_1k=if(sum_tokens>0, round(1000*sum_cost/sum_tokens,6), 0)
| streamstats window=4 current=t global=f last(cost_per_1k) AS prev_cp1k BY tenant_id deployment_name provider model_alias
| eval cost_step_ratio=if(isnotnull(prev_cp1k) AND prev_cp1k>0.000001, round(cost_per_1k/prev_cp1k,3), 0)
| join type=left max=0 tenant_id deployment_name provider model_alias
    [| inputlookup llm_approved_model_versions.csv
     | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(tenant_id)))
     | eval deployment_name=lower(trim(toString(deployment_name)))
     | eval provider=lower(trim(toString(provider)))
     | eval model_alias=lower(trim(toString(model_alias)))
     | fields tenant_id deployment_name provider model_alias approved_snapshot_regex max_shadow_traffic_pct alias_pin_required owner_team notes ]
| eval approved_snapshot_regex=coalesce(approved_snapshot_regex,".*")
| eval max_shadow_traffic_pct=tonumber(coalesce(max_shadow_traffic_pct,100))
| eval alias_pin_required=coalesce(alias_pin_required,"no")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned_route")
| eval allow_match=if(match(ver_max, approved_snapshot_regex),1,0)
| eval mismatch_allowlist=if(allow_match==0 AND approved_snapshot_regex!=".*",1,0)
| eval floating_alias=if(alias_pin_required=="yes" AND match(model_alias,"^(gpt-4o|gpt-4-turbo|claude-3-sonnet|gemini-2\.0-flash|gemini-1\.5-pro)$"),1,0)
| eval shadow_breach=if(max_shadow_traffic_pct<99 AND traffic_share_pct>(max_shadow_traffic_pct*1.5),1,0)
| join type=left max=0 tenant_id
    [| tstats count AS change_touchpoints FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Change.user
     | rename Change.user AS tenant_id
     | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(tenant_id))) ]
| eval severity=case(mismatch_allowlist==1 OR floating_alias==1, "critical", shadow_breach==1 OR flip_flop==1 OR cost_step_ratio>1.35, "high", cost_step_ratio>1.15 OR ver_dc>1, "medium", traffic_share_pct>8 AND traffic_share_pct<=max_shadow_traffic_pct*1.2, "low", true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical" AND floating_alias==1, "pin_dated_snapshots_freeze_alias_routes_update_gateway_config_repo", severity=="critical" AND mismatch_allowlist==1, "rollback_traffic_refresh_allowlist_open_model_governance_ticket", severity=="high" AND flip_flop==1, "correlate_openai_system_fingerprint_and_provider_release_calendar_stabilize_route", severity=="high" AND shadow_breach==1, "rebalance_litellm_weight_map_validate_azure_deployment_and_bedrock_profile_targets", severity=="high", "diff_prompt_templates_evaluate_safety_regressions_before_cost_chasing", true(), "continue_hourly_version_ledger_exports_for_audit")
| table _time tenant_id owner_team provider deployment_name region model_alias ver_min ver_max ver_dc traffic_share_pct cost_per_1k cost_step_ratio flip_flop mismatch_allowlist floating_alias shadow_breach severity recommended_action lanes change_touchpoints
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Change.command) AS change_cmd latest(Change.object) AS change_obj latest(Change.action) AS change_action FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Change.user Change.dest | rename Change.user AS actor Change.dest AS change_target | table actor change_target change_cmd change_action change_obj
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by tenant_id, deployment_name, and model_alias with drilldowns to multisearch lanes, fp_vals, allowlist regex rows, and Change touchpoints; timeline of ver_max and cost_step_ratio; single-value tiles for traffic_share_pct and flip_flop density.

## Known False Positives

Planned multi-snapshot evaluations that intentionally alternate between two approved board baselines for offline scoring can elevate ver_dc and flip_flop without implying production drift; tag tenant_id with eval_suite metadata and downgrade non-production severities. Provider-announced maintenance that rotates system_fingerprint while model_alias stays pinned may trigger informational flip_flop until notes in llm_approved_model_versions.csv acknowledge the window. Azure deployment swaps executed under change control can move ver_max legitimately; require CAB correlation before paging application owners. Gemini regional failovers that change publisher strings without altering approved capability tiers may look like mismatches until regex patterns include alternation groups for regional publishers. Stale allowlist rows that omit newly blessed snapshots cause mismatch_allowlist spikes that reflect spreadsheet lag, not abuse; automate lookup pulls from Git. Brief LiteLLM cache warm-up phases can skew traffic_share_pct until steady state; compare against five trailing buckets. Duplicate HEC submissions can inflate req_count; dedupe on request_id. Cost_step_ratio swings from currency normalization or tax line changes belong in UC-13.4.2 reconciliation, not emergency rollback here. Canary tenants that deliberately float aliases for research should set alias_pin_required=no in the lookup. tstats Change summaries absent acceleration return empty change_touchpoints; repair datamodel acceleration rather than muting detections.

- [LiteLLM Documentation — Proxy server](https://docs.litellm.ai/docs/proxy/quick_start)
- [Splunk Lantern — Monitor home](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
- [OpenAI API reference — Chat Completions](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/)
